rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERED TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY** IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY OWYANG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel 1) Responses To Form Interrogatories-General Set One; 2) Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And 3) Deemed Admissions To Requests For Admissions, Set One And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Now before the Court is Petitioners Crystal Owyang and Jason Owyang's Motion to Compel (1) Response to Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; (2) Responses to Request for Production of Documents, Set One; (3) Deemed Admissions to Requests for Admissions, Set One, and for monetary sanctions. Petitioners seek this relief under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2030.290, 2031.300, and 2033.280, respectively. The parties are advised, going forward, to bring separate motions when they are seeking relief under separate and distinct sections of the Code of Civil Procedure against separate individuals, rather than filing a single omnibus motion on all outstanding discovery. No opposition to this motion was filed with the court. No reply or notice of non-opposition was filed with the court. There is no indication before the court that any of the requested discovery has been produced. Under CCP 2030.290(a), "If a party to whom interrogatories are directed fails to serve a timely response, [] [t]he party to whom the interrogatories are directed waives any right to exercise the option to produce writings under Section 2030.230, as well as any objection to the interrogatories, including one based on privilege or on the protection for work product under Chapter 4 (commencing with Section 2018.010)." A motion to compel responses to unanswered interrogatories or inspection demands is not subject to a statutory time limit, and the propounding party does not have to demonstrate either good cause or that it satisfied a "meet-and-confer" requirement. ?(Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 390; Code of Civ. Proc. section 2030.290; Leach v. Sup. Ct (1980) 111 Cal. App. 3d 902, 905-906.) No separate statement is required. (See CRC, Rule 3.1345(b)). The motion must show only: (1) a set of interrogatories was properly served on the opposing party; (2) the time to respond has expired; and (3) no response of any kind has been served. (Leach v. Sup. Ct (1980) 111 Cal. App. 3d 902, 905-906.) Petitioners' Motion to Compel Response to Form Interrogatories - General, Set One, as to Respondent Susan Owyang Varela is GRANTED. The motion shows Varela was properly served Form Interrogatories - General, Set One by mail on July 1, 2022, that the time to respond has expired, and there has been no response of any kind. (See Collins Decl. Exh. C.) Petitioners' Motion to Compel Response to Form Interrogatories - General, Set One, as to Respondent Elaine Owyang Jaymot is GRANTED. The motion shows Jaymot was properly served Form Interrogatories - General, Set One by mail on July 1, 2022, that the time to respond has expired, and there has been no response of any kind. (See Collins Decl. Exh. D.) Under CCP 2031.300(a), "If a party to whom a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling is directed fails to serve a timely response to it, [] [t]he party to whom the demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling is directed waives any objection to the demand, including one based on privilege or on the protection for work product under Chapter 4 (commencing with Section 2018.010). The motion to compel response to a demand for inspection must show (1) an inspection demand was properly served on the opposing party, (2) the time to respond has expired, and (3) no response of any kind has been served. (Leach v. Sup. Ct (1980) 111 Cal. App. 3d 902, 905-906.) No separate statement is required. (See CRC, Rule 3.1345(b)) There is no statutory "meet-and-confer" requirement. (Code of Civ. Proc. section 2031.300(b).) [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERED TO CONTRA COSTA COUNTY** IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY OWYANG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel 1) Responses To Form Interrogatories-General Set One; 2) Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And 3) Deemed Admissions To Requests For Admissions, Set One And For Sanctions (*Added To Calendar For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Purposes Only*)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] Petitioners' Motion to Compel Responses Request for Production of Documents, Set One, as to Respondent Susan Owyang Varela is GRANTED. The motion shows Varela was properly served Request for Production of Documents, Set One by mail on July 1, 2022, that the time to respond has expired, and there has been no response of any kind. (See Collins Decl. Exh. E.) Petitioners' Motion to Compel Responses Request for Production of Documents, Set One, as to Respondent Elaine Owyang Jaymot is GRANTED. The motion shows Jaymot was properly served Request for Production of Documents, Set One by mail on July 1, 2022, that the time to respond has expired, and there has been no response of any kind. (See Collins Decl. Exh. F.) Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280(a), "If a party to whom requests for admission [RFAs] are directed fails to serve a timely response, [] [t]he party to whom the requests for admission are directed waives any objection to the requests, including one based on privilege or on the protection for work product under Chapter 4 (commencing with Section 2018.010)." The motion to deem admissions admitted must show (1) a set of requests for admission was properly served on the opposing party; (2) the time to respond has expired; and (3) no response of any kind has been served. (CCP sections 2033.250, 2033.280.) No separate statement is required. (CRC 3.1345(b).) The moving party is not required to have met and conferred (Code of Civ. Proc. section 2033.280(a); St. Mary v. Sup. Ct (2014) 223 Cal. App. 4th 762, 777-778.) The court must order the RFAs deemed admitted if the moving party has made the required showing (Code of Civ. Proc. sections 2033.220, 2033.280(c), St. Mary v. Sup. Ct (2014) 223 Cal. App. 4th 762, 778.) Petitioners' motion to deem Requests for Admission, Set One, served on Respondent Susan Owyang Varela, admitted is GRANTED. The motion shows Varela was properly served Petitioners' Requests for Admissions, Set One by mail on July 1, 2022, that the time to respond has expired, and there has been no response of any kind. (See Collins Decl. Exh. A.) Petitioners' motion to deem Requests for Admission, Set One, served on Respondent Elaine Owyang Jaymot, admitted is GRANTED. The motion shows Jaymot was properly served Petitioners' Requests for Admissions, Set One by mail on July 1, 2022, that the time to respond has expired, and there has been no response of any kind. (See Collins Decl. Exh. B.) Petitioners seek monetary sanctions under the respective relevant subsections of Code of Civil Procedure sections 2030.290, 2031.300, and 2033.280. Request for sanctions under this statutory authority is DENIED, as there was no opposition to this motion. However, the court imposes, on its own motion, monetary sanctions in the total amount of $960 on Respondents Varela and Jaymot jointly, under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010(d) and 2023.030(a), due to the Respondents' misuse of the discovery process by failing to respond to an authorized method of discovery. On the record currently before the court, there is no showing that either Respondent acted with substantial justification in failing to respond to the propounded discovery, or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Respondents Susan Owyang Varela and Elain Owyang Jaymot are jointly ordered to pay Petitioners Crystal Owyang and Jason Owyang a total of $960 in monetary sanctions, within 10 days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. [Part 2 of 3]
